A three-month creative and performance plan engineered to grow Cielito from 83.2K to 250K followers, and lift sales by 30%+ month over month.

01 The Brand

A wardrobe written in soft light.

Cielito is an Egyptian women's fashion house designed for women who treat dressing as ritual. Every piece is a small chapter in a longer life. A summer morning. A Friday brunch. A walk through old Cairo at golden hour.

This quarter, we sharpen that story. A content engine where creator-led trust meets editorial craft, founder voice meets AI imagination, and every piece of beauty is measured by what it moves.

i.

Refined femininity

Soft, modern, considered. Romance without nostalgia.

ii.

Made for moments

Each piece is anchored to an occasion the woman is dressing for. Never abstract product alone.

iii.

Egyptian craft, global gaze

Local sensibility, international finish. Cairo as a setting, not a limitation.

A full women's wardrobe · across nineteen categories

02 Objectives & KPIs

Two numbers. One quarter. A measurable arc from awareness to purchase.

Sales lift · Month 01
+0%
Baseline target. Driven by paid amplification of high-intent creator content and conversion-led product storytelling.
Year-end follower goal
0K
From a current base of 83.2K. Quarter contributes 70 to 90K new followers; remaining months sustain the curve into December.
Active creators
0
10 top-tier creators, 6 Basma Mohamed pieces, 5 founder-led with Lolwa. Plus a layer of micro-influencers under the 40K EGP allocation.
Primary · Sales

Lift sales 30% in month one, accelerating each month after.

Sales is the lead indicator. Every content piece is briefed to either drive immediate consideration, social proof, or post-click conversion. Branding outputs are built to inherit that conversion weight, not to replace it.

+30%
Month 01
+45%
Month 02
+60%
Month 03
Secondary · Growth

Reach 250K followers by year-end.

Driven by reach-led content: founder storytelling, AI editorial imagery, and creator co-signs that travel beyond the existing audience. Sustained, not spiked.

83.2K
Today
~165K
End Q1
250K
Dec 2026
03 Strategy Overview

Four pillars, one voice.

The content engine runs on four distinct content sources. Each carries a different job, but all share the same editorial finish. Sales-led creators do the heavy commercial lifting. Founder storytelling and AI visuals carry the brand mythology that makes Cielito worth following in the first place.

i.

UGC creators

Real women in real lives. Trust signal that converts. Highest weight in paid amplification.

Role · Sales engine
ii.

Influencer co-signs

Top-tier creators and Basma Mohamed extending Cielito into adjacent style audiences with editorial-quality outputs.

Role · Reach + credibility
iii.

Founder storytelling

Lolwa as the human centre of the brand: origin, taste, conviction. Filmed in places that feel like Cielito.

Role · Mythology + retention
iv.

AI editorial

Hyperreal, romantic, sky-soft visual layer that gives Cielito a fashion-magazine surface without runway-sized budgets.

Role · Brand world + scroll-stopping
Sales-led, brand alwaysUGC · Influencer · Founder · AICielito 2026Style is a moment Sales-led, brand alwaysUGC · Influencer · Founder · AICielito 2026Style is a moment
04 Content Strategy

Twelve content directions. Sales first, brand always.

Each idea is sequenced by primary objective. The first half of the quarter leans heavily into UGC and creator-led conversion content; the back half introduces founder storytelling and AI editorial pieces to build reach and brand love. No captions or scripts. Only direction, hooks, and intent.

Sales Brand Reel Static UGC
01
Her Cielito Moment. Hook · "The pieces I keep reaching for."

ConceptTen women, ten moments. A serial UGC drop where each creator pairs Cielito with the occasion they were dressing for: a brunch, a coffee meeting, a first date. The frame stays human; the product is felt, not sold.

Why it performsHighest-trust format on Reels. Built for paid amplification because the creator's voice does the persuading; we just place media weight behind the best three.

DirectionVertical, raw smartphone aesthetic, soft daylight, a single styling reveal per piece. No brand graphics on screen.

Type
UGC · Reel series
Objective
Sales
Tags
SalesUGC
02
From Cart to Closet. Hook · "What just arrived."

ConceptAn aesthetic unboxing series across five micro-creators. Hands, packaging, the unwrapping ritual. The post-purchase moment as a hero scene.

Why it performsCloses the conversion loop. People buying for the first time want to see what arrives. High view-through, high save-rate, and seeds pre-purchase trust for cold audiences.

DirectionSoft window light, hands only or partial face. Tactile sound design. End on the styled look. Never on a brand stamp.

Type
UGC · Reel
Objective
Sales · Conversion
Tags
SalesUGC
03
How I Style It. Hook · "One piece. Three looks."

ConceptBasma Mohamed at home, styling a single Cielito piece (e.g. a dress, a pair of sandals, a summer set) into three distinct looks: workday, evening, weekend. Treats the product as a styling ingredient, not a single-use object.

Why it performsResolves the silent buyer objection ("where would I wear it"). One of Basma's six contracted pieces; her audience trusts her styling instinct.

DirectionNatural lighting, mirror reveal, soft handheld camera. Three quick cuts, no on-screen prices.

Type
Influencer · Reel
Objective
Sales · Engagement
Tags
SalesInfluencer
04
Streets of Cairo. Hook · "The city, in Cielito."

ConceptBasma walking through scenic Cairo locations: Zamalek alleys, El Moez Street, Garden City. The look travels through real architecture; the city becomes the runway.

Why it performsReach format. Egyptian audiences engage strongly with Cairo-as-backdrop content; international audiences read it as fashion-editorial. Low cost, high ceiling.

DirectionCinematic walking shots, golden-hour palette, ambient city sound. No voiceover. Single cohesive look per video.

Type
Influencer · Reel
Objective
Reach · Brand
Tags
BrandInfluencer
05
The Piece That Started It All. Hook · "Before there was Cielito."

ConceptFounder origin reel with Lolwa. A walk through Al Khayamiya or El Moez Street, narrating the moment the brand began. Following the @getpalma reference, planned for May to anchor the founder-led arc.

Why it performsFounder content is the single highest-retention format for fashion brands. It transforms followers into supporters, and supporters into repeat buyers.

DirectionCinematic, slow b-roll, golden hour, Lolwa speaking softly. Real location sound. The product appears once, in close-up, near the end.

Type
Founder · Reel
Objective
Brand · Trust
Tags
BrandFounder
06
The Founder's Edit. Hook · "The five I'd choose."

ConceptLolwa hand-picks her five favourite pieces of the season and explains why. What occasion, what woman, what feeling. Editorial home shoot, not a haul.

Why it performsTranslates founder taste into a shoppable narrative. Pre-qualifies high-margin pieces. Naturally generates carousel posts, story sequences, and a hero reel from one shoot day.

DirectionSoft handheld, neutral home interior, one piece at a time on a quiet pedestal. Lolwa's voiceover sits underneath, never on camera.

Type
Founder · Reel + Story
Objective
Sales · Brand
Tags
SalesFounder
07
Behind the Stitch. Hook · "Cielito, made closely."

ConceptAtelier POV. Macro on materials, fabric tones, hands working a hem. Quietly humanises the craft and earns the price point across the full ready-to-wear and footwear range.

Why it performsPremium positioning. Justifies cost without ever discussing it. Gives the algorithm a different visual signal from creator content, which is useful for discovery.

DirectionASMR-led sound design, no music or soft piano only. Natural workshop light. No faces, only hands and material.

Type
Brand · Reel
Objective
Brand · Premium
Tags
Brand
08
AI Editorial · Moments of Style. Hook · "Cielito as a sky."

ConceptAn AI-generated dream campaign. Hyperreal romantic settings of clouds, soft fields, and warm coastlines, paired with Cielito hero pieces (e.g. dresses, sandals, summer sets). Plays directly on the meaning of "cielito" (little sky).

Why it performsVisual-only branding equity at near-zero cost. Strong save-rate. Frees us to publish "campaign-grade" imagery monthly without a production budget. Five static creatives planned.

DirectionInspired by the @cordis reference: minimal type, off-white frames, soft grain. AI imagery should feel painted, not synthetic. Guide the model with editorial fashion references.

Type
AI · Static + Reel
Objective
Brand · Reach
Tags
BrandAI
09
Piece of the Week. Hook · "This week, only."

ConceptA single hero piece (e.g. a dress, a pair of heels, a summer set) styled three ways in one carousel. Editorial product spotlight, weekly cadence, conversion-led. @cordis-inspired layout system.

Why it performsRecurring format trains the audience to expect a weekly drop signal. Drives repeat profile visits and gives Stories a built-in rhythm. Strong for both sales and follower retention.

DirectionMagazine-grade still life, AI-generated backdrops where useful, off-white type. Each carousel ends on a quiet "shop the look" frame.

Type
Static · Carousel
Objective
Sales
Tags
SalesStatic
10
Inside Cielito HQ. Hook · "A day, from inside."

ConceptFive employee-generated content pieces. Behind-the-scenes from real team members: packing orders, choosing fabrics, picking the next shoot location.

Why it performsBuilds the small-business intimacy that established fashion brands lose. Cheap to produce, high engagement, and trains the audience to feel ownership of Cielito's growth story.

DirectionPhone-first, vertical, casual. Captioned in a quiet brand font. No staging. Only what actually happens that day.

Type
EGC · Story / Reel
Objective
Brand · Trust
Tags
BrandEGC
11
24 Hours in Cielito. Hook · "From morning, to last call."

ConceptAn influencer wears Cielito across four occasions in one day: work, lunch, errands, evening. Time-stamped reel structure that lets one woman wear multiple Cielito categories (e.g. a blouse, a dress, sandals) inside a single video.

Why it performsResolves multiple buying objections in 30 seconds: comfort, versatility, day-to-night. Time-stamped format reads as utility, not advertising.

DirectionCinematic but light, fast cuts, real environments only. Time-stamps as small editorial type bottom-left. No transitions; just smart cuts.

Type
Influencer · Reel
Objective
Sales
Tags
SalesInfluencer
12
The First Drop Test. Hook · "Limited. Before anyone else."

ConceptA controlled limited-release teaser used at the end of Month 02 / start of Month 03. Story-led countdown, tease imagery, early-access for the most engaged audience segment. Works across any product category we choose to drop (e.g. summer sandals, a small heels capsule, a limited summer set).

Why it performsManufactures urgency without discounting. Tests demand on a small SKU set, generates user-generated reaction content for free, and seeds a repeatable drop format for the rest of the year.

DirectionDark teaser frames, white-space reveals, countdown tickers in stories. The actual product reveal is quiet and unhurried.

Type
Story · Reel
Objective
Sales · Urgency
Tags
Sales
Visual Benchmarks

The references we're aiming at.

A pinboard of internal Cielito visuals that set the tone for the five static creatives, plus two Instagram references for the wider aesthetic and storytelling direction of the quarter.

07 Cielito · Internal Pinboard nine pins
Cielito Must Havespin · 01
Summer Timepin · 02
It Mules · annotatedpin · 03
Mules in Beigepin · 04
Editorial Layoutpin · 05
Polaroid · It Mulespin · 06
Cielito on the Gopin · 07
Shell Slipperpin · 08
Portraitpin · 09
moodboard · note
Soft cream, hero cutouts, tactile paper.

The five static creatives this quarter should read like editorial pages from a small fashion magazine. The visual rules: warm neutral grounds, single-piece focus, hand-finished type, a quiet sense of humour.

03 External · Instagram References three links
Aesthetic · Vibe
Reference · 01

The aesthetic vibe.

@cordis

The general visual language we are aiming at. Soft, considered, editorial. Cordis treats every product post like a magazine page: restraint, negative space, warm light, real moments. The reference covers tone more than format.

View the post on Instagram
Carousel · White
Reference · 02

The white-background carousels.

@cordis · second post

The exact reference for our static carousels and Piece of the Week posts. Off-white frames, single hero piece per slide, magazine-grade type, a single accent colour pulled from the product. This is the layout system we mirror, scaled to Cielito's full ready-to-wear and footwear range.

View the carousel on Instagram
Storytelling · Founder
Reference · 03

The founder tone.

@getpalma

The narrative tone for Lolwa's May origin film. Founder speaking, slow b-roll, real location, no overproduction. Quiet, intimate, unforced. The whole point of the May arc is that Cielito's customer feels she's met Lolwa, not watched an ad.

View the reel on Instagram
05 Creator Plan

A roster built for trust.

Twenty-one core pieces this quarter, structured by the role each source plays. Top-tier creators handle reach and credibility. Basma Mohamed delivers the editorial layer. Lolwa carries the brand soul. EGC and AI extend the engine without raising production cost.

10·

Top-tier creators.

A curated mix of UGC creators and lifestyle influencers. Selected for audience overlap with Cielito's customer profile, comment-quality, and creative independence, not raw follower count.

Mix · UGC + Influencer · Sales-led
06·

Basma Mohamed.

Six contracted pieces. A balance of outdoor cinematic work in Cairo's most photogenic locations and softer at-home styling content. The bridge between editorial and accessible.

Outdoor + Home · Editorial
05·

Lolwa, founder.

Five storytelling videos shot in aesthetic Cairo locations like Al Khayamiya and El Moez Street. Plan anchors in May, following the @getpalma reference.

Founder · Storytelling
05·

Static + AI.

Five static creatives in the @cordis-inspired editorial system. Some shot conventionally, others built with AI-generated romantic environments, all built off the internal moodboard reference.

Static · Editorial + AI
05·

Employee content.

Five EGC pieces from the team: packing, fittings, daily life inside Cielito. Lowest cost, highest authenticity layer in the mix.

EGC · Behind-the-scenes
40K EGP

Micro-influencer layer.

A 40K EGP allocation to seed Cielito across a wider net of micro-influencers, under-50K-follower creators with high engagement and tight community trust. Distributed across small product gifts and modest paid placements, prioritising creators whose feed already reads like Cielito.

Allocation logic
8 to 10 micro-creators~3 to 5K EGP each
Product gifts in kindlayered on top
Paid amplification of best 3retainer pool
06 Monthly Plan

Three acts. Each one earns the next.

The quarter is sequenced, not balanced. Month 01 is built to perform, fast. Month 02 introduces the brand world that retention depends on. Month 03 scales what worked and tests the first drop format.

April
01
Theme · Visibility & conversion

Open the quarter with momentum.

Front-load UGC creator content and paid amplification. Establish the weekly Piece of the Week cadence. Launch first Basma at-home styling piece. Hold founder content for May, per plan.

  • 4 UGC ReelsHer Cielito Moment series, paid-amplified
  • 2 Basma pieces1 home styling, 1 outdoor Cairo walk
  • 4 Piece of the Week carouselsStatic editorial, conversion-led
  • 2 EGC clipsStories + lightweight reels
  • 2 AI editorial stillsFor grid + paid creative
Target
+30% sales
May
02
Theme · Story & brand world

Introduce the founder layer.

Roll out the Lolwa storytelling arc, anchored by the origin reel filmed in Al Khayamiya / El Moez. Sustain UGC volume but shift weight toward founder + AI editorial pieces to lift reach and follower acquisition.

  • 2 to 3 Lolwa reelsOrigin + Founder's Edit (per @getpalma reference)
  • 2 Basma pieces1 outdoor cinematic, 1 styling
  • 3 UGC ReelsFrom cart to closet, unboxing-led
  • 4 Piece of the Week carouselsContinued cadence
  • 2 AI editorial piecesMoments of Style series
Target
+45% sales · +30K followers
June
03
Theme · Scale & first drop

Scale what works, test the drop.

Activate the remainder of the top-10 creator roster. Launch the First Drop Test as a controlled urgency campaign. Layer in micro-influencer seeding from the 40K EGP pool. Conversion is the lead metric; reach is the byproduct.

  • 4 to 5 top creator piecesMixed UGC + influencer outputs
  • 2 Basma piecesFinal outdoor + styling deliveries
  • 2 Lolwa piecesFounder's Edit + drop intro
  • 1 First Drop TestStory-led countdown + reel reveal
  • Micro-influencer seeding40K EGP layer activated
Target
+60% sales · ~165K followers
07 Budget Allocation

Where the money goes.

The 40K EGP allocation covers the UGC creators and the micro-influencer seeding layer. No additional budget is added on top. The split below is how the pool is distributed inside that scope. All other production layers (founder time, Basma's contracted pieces, EGC, AI editorial work) sit outside this budget and are handled in kind, in trade, or against existing brand resources.

Disclosed Budget
40K EGP

Splits across the UGC creator roster and a layer of micro-influencer seeding. Both groups are compensated cash plus product. The remaining brand pillars (Basma, Lolwa, EGC, AI editorial) are produced outside this allocation.
